mnemonics: CRASH for ANOVA Types: Completely Randomized, Randomized Block, ANCOVA, Split-Plot, Hierarchical
keyPoints: ANOVA tests if the means of three or more groups are statistically different.
The F-statistic is the ratio of variance between groups to variance within groups.
A p-value less than the significance level (e.g., 0.05) leads to rejecting the null hypothesis that all group means are equal.
commonMistakes: Using ANOVA to compare only two groups (a t-test is more appropriate).
Failing to check assumptions like normality and homogeneity of variances before running the test.
Concluding which specific groups are different after a significant ANOVA without performing a post-hoc test.
